facts.statement: Honeybees are social insects that live in colonies of tens of thousands.
questions: What type of insects are honeybees?
How many honeybees live in a colony?
Do honeybees live alone or in groups?

facts.statement: A honeybee colony has a single queen.
questions: How many queens are in a honeybee colony?
Does a honeybee colony have multiple queens?
What is the structure of a honeybee colony?

facts.statement: Honeybee workers are female and present in large numbers in a colony.
questions: What are female honeybees called?
How many workers does a honeybee colony have?
What makes up the majority of a honeybee colony?

facts.statement: Honeybee drones are male and present seasonally in a colony.
questions: What are male honeybees called?
When are drones present in a honeybee colony?
Are male honeybees always in the hive?

facts.statement: The waggle dance is a movement honeybee workers use to communicate the location of food.
questions: How do honeybee workers communicate food locations?
What is the waggle dance?
How do honeybees tell each other where to find food?

facts.statement: Honeybees produce honey from flower nectar.
questions: What do honeybees produce from flower nectar?
How do honeybees make honey?
What is the source material for honey?
Why do honeybees store honey?
What do honeybees do with the honey they produce?
How do honeybees prepare for winter?

facts.statement: Honeybees pollinate plants as they gather nectar.
questions: How do honeybees pollinate plants?
What happens when honeybees gather nectar?
Do honeybees pollinate flowers?

facts.statement: Honeybees are vital to agriculture because they pollinate plants.
questions: Why are honeybees important to agriculture?
Are honeybees essential for farming?
What role do honeybees play in food production?

facts.statement: The honeybee queen is the only bee that lays fertilized eggs.
questions: Which bee lays fertilized eggs in a colony?
Does the queen bee lay eggs?
Who reproduces in a honeybee colony?
